text:
I had boxes for recycling in the front seat of my car. Always multitasking, I was changing to sunglasses & checking my phone at stoplights. I hurriedly tossed phone & glasses case into one of the boxes then forgot about them.  I launched the pile of boxes into the cardboard dumpster, & didn’t realize what I’d done until I got home.

I quickly called <PERSON>’s to see if they could help in any way. My call was answered after <ORGANIZATION> many employees who were still there sprung into action to help out.

Two gentlemen literally did a dumpster dive to find my phone while another employee kept calling my number so they could hear the vibration (my phone is always on silent mode).

These folks went above & beyond to locate my phone, all the time communicating with me where they were in the process. I have nothing but high praises for the employees of <ORGANIZATION> am eternally grateful!

text:
Our recycle hasn’t been picked up for a month due to holidays and bad weather. But this week, we were supposed to have it picked up. Trash day came and went, and our neighborhood’s recycling carts were still full. Another day came, and I called them in the afternoon to see what is going on. They said that they were aware of our situation, and that they promised someone would be by that day to pick it up. Well now it’s Saturday, and I just checked our cart and it’s still full. They are closed for the weekend, so it might be another two weeks before they get to us.

I’m wondering why we’re paying them for a service they aren’t actually providing. 🤔 I might find a local who offers to haul your recycle for you. I’ve seen a number of people offering their services in lieu of the situation with <PERSON>’s.

text:
Big thanks to company management. You failed to pick up the extra garbage last week. We called last Friday; we were told you would pick up that Friday or Monday. Your company failed to meet that commitment.  Called Monday afternoon you informed us you would be out Tuesday. That also didn't happen. Called Wednesday morning, you said you would pick up on our regular scheduled day, Thursday. History proving otherwise, you showed up, picked up the extra garbage and all the recyclables. So, congrats on that.
If I was late paying my bill, and  failed three times in a row to pay when I had committed to do so.... What would your company policy be on that?

text: It's unfortunate when an area is contracted with a garbage service and you can't hire another company to service your area - businesses get lazy and the residents seem to get the short end of the stick. We had some poor weather recently that delayed our garbage pick up, but rather than have it picked up a day or two later as conditions improved, <ORGANIZATION>'s opted to just skip their duties this week and allow all the garbages and recycles in our area to overflow into the streets to be spread around by the local crows. Their "solution" was just to put extra garbage bags on the road and they'll get to them next week, or in two weeks in the case of the recycle - to make things worse, the afternoon of the day when they said they'd pick them up we get a call saying they are delaying the service another day. I drive for work and can comfortably say that the conditions all over the city and surrounding areas are perfectly fine but since there's no repercussion to skipping the service, why not just push it off another day right? To add insult to injury I'm sure that they will be charging in full come the next billing period, despite not following through on services paid for. Seems like lazy and underwhelming business practice to me but what do you expect when they're the only ones allowed to service an area. No competition = poor service.

text: My pickup was January 3rd.  Over night the snow on my property melted and several inches of water accumulated around my garbage can next to the street.  When I came out to retrieve the empty can I noticed that the driver had moved it out of the ankle deep water and sat it back down on the other side of my driveway on higher ground so I wouldn’t have to wade through water to get it.  I am an older man in my 70’s and deeply appreciate this stellar service. I want that drivers supervisor and managers to realize what a special employee they have. Thank you very much from the bottom of my heart.

text: We contacted <PERSON>'s three weeks prior to moving (staying with-in their service area) and were assured that our garbage and recycle would be taken care of at both our old and new addresses. Our garbage and recycle pick-up day was the day before we moved and <PERSON>'s skipped us. When we called to have them return, we were told they would come the following week but we reiterated that we were moving and the ended up coming several days post-move.  We moved into our new house and no bins had been delivered, as we had been promised there would be by the time we arrived. It took three weeks and multiple phone calls to have bins delivered and then another two weeks to be picked up. AND they tried to charge us for all of that time! I am now currently on-hold as we do not use our yard-waste bin and I would like to discontinue that aspect of the service. I've called intermittently for two weeks and haven't made it past caller number 12 after over an hour on hold. If we had the bandwidth to go to the dump, we would!

text:
I would like to make others aware, in case you are dealing with a similar situation, that <PERSON>'s Disposal has been charging us overages nearly every week.
The overages started in Dec 2019 and have been consistently accruing. I received notification of suspended service in June 2020, due to the fact that our auto pay doesn't cover overages, and contacted the company to resolve the situation. At that time, the representative reversed the charges and stated that the driver would take pictures to provide proof of the overages.
We just received another notice of suspended service - Sep 2020 - stating that we had 7 more charges since June. The supervisor, <PERSON>, said that the driver has not taken any photos nor is he required to provide any proof or reasoning for the overages. The company is relying on the driver's word (<PERSON> also stated that he "knows the driver personally") and made it clear that they aren't interest in the customers word. I am expecting a call from the driver's supervisor for further discussions.
For the record, I make sure my containers are spaced appropriately, that the lids are completely closed and that there are no extra bags next to the container. Yes, I am the same person complaining about dog poop in my cans. I have no idea why garbage has been giving us such a problem.
Hopefully no one else is having to deal with this.

text: This place is amazing. They have taken care of my family for years now. Construction debris boxes, yard waste boxes, storage boxes, and the weekly cans. Aside from what they offer they're very clear on how to put out your cans, how full they should be, and even the space between them with overhead clearance. The reason I'm writing this review is because the awful reviews I've seen. People that want to harm a companies reputation because they couldn't follow the clear and understandable guidelines they have to keep their services as safe, and on time as possible. I forgot to put out my cans over labor day week because I thought it was Wednesday instead of Thursday. My wife realized as the truck was coming through to service our neighborhood. She rushed to get them out. The driver saw and didn't just keep going. He didn't even sit and wait for us to correct our mistake shaking his head in annoyance. He truly showed his sense of pride and custimer service by getting out, helping my wife get the cans out, and servicing us. AND IT WAS MY FAULT! Seriously he could've just gone on with his day, and it wouldn't have effected him in any way. If anything it slowed him down! Maybe he hit more traffic... Or more red lights, or a larger line dumping at the landfill. This company is gold. I would highly recommend them for any services they offer. If you can follow directions and understand the systematic way they operate to keep everyone safe in such a drastically dangerous job they do daily... They'll never let you down. Thank you <PERSON>'s. We are customers for life.

text: When looking up <ORGANIZATION>, to locate the website for our weekly schedule, I noticed that they had so many negative reviews.  I feel the need to report my service honestly, and it has not been perfect but nothing ever is.  The service has been fair, actually above what we would expect for such a valuable and obviously thankless service.  This is tough work, especially after reading some of these reviews.  We have just not had the same experience.  We have had moments where something occurred in the schedule.  We would call and someone would come out.  There were times when we made mistakes too and someone let us know, all good.  I cannot complain at all, which is why I give them 5 Stars.  Not sure if there are different divisions but <PERSON>'s has been an honorable business for us this past 10 years, and I wanted to put in my two cents amongst all of the negative ones.  Also, remember that COVID times are not normal times.  We do not know what new normal is so have some decency and tolerance on both sides.  We all may be having bad days.

text: Hard working friendly drivers. People don’t give these guys enough credit for the service they provide us. Just saw on <ORGANIZATION> that they are teaming up with some local police to watch out for suspicious people and activities in our neighborhoods! HOW AWESOME IS THAT! I don’t know how anyone couldn’t love hearing that. I see mostly negative reviews here which i can only assume is because the thousands of other people who don’t write reviews, take the garbage company for granted and don’t think about taking the time to stop and appreciate what they do. These guys are up against a lot of odds day to day doing what they do. They have to watch out for our kids and pets playing in our neighborhoods, impatient drivers, developing relationships with us homeowners and addressing our concerns or questions, not to mention having to deal with the task of driving the heavy technical vehicles they drive. I personally have never had a single issue with my garbage, recycling, or yard waste drivers. Always very friendly and polite and they never fail to give a smile and a wave if I’m out in the yard when they come by. Thanks for everything you guys do and working most of the holidays that the rest of us get to enjoy at home with our families!

Or wait a week cause you showed up at 7 am when you usually get here at 10. I tried calling for customer service but was hung up on. I can't wait to move so I can find a better trash collector.

text: I am writing a review because I needed to call customer service for a problem and was dreading it.  In the past, my calls resulted in long wait times and ended without satisfactory conclusions.  I have not called in quite a long time.  My yard waste was not picked up on it's normal day and I had to call and report it.  Customer service was very helpful and pleasant.  She apologized (wow) and said they had a new driver yesterday and mine was not the first call.  I was impressed that they owned up to a mistake.  Human error is understandable.  I do not have a problem with that until someone adamantly denies it and makes me out to be the bad guy.  Not the case here.  Was pleasantly surprised!  Not sure if <PERSON>'s has done the program....but I definitely felt like I got the "pickle".  Thanks.

text: Cans are emptied on the schedule, cans are placed back where I left them (not tossed about as many others have said).  Lids are inside the can... thus keeping them from blowing down the street.  If I have "extra" containers they are emptied and I am billed for it... just like the agreement states.  I get exactly what I pay for, and if there's a fire truck blocking the road so they can't get to the cans (yup, it happened) when I call to ask they have a full explanation.  I'd give 5 stars if they came back to get my garbage, but I did have to wait until the next week for pick-up.  They took double... but I only have the two cans to put garbage in.  Oh Well, the garbage was taken away... just as I expected when I signed up for service

text: Last year they picked up our Xmas tree for free and disposed of it. This year (like last year) I cut the tree in half to make it easier for them to pickup. Come to find out on my next bill I am charged twice for a "tree fee." Apparently I am not within city limits they say so therefore the charge is valid? Okay I get that, but they still wouldn't refund me at least half of the charge back for trying to make it easier for their workers to pickup. <ORGANIZATION>!

text: Almost every week the lid to my garbage can has just been tossed wherever. Recently the lid just disappeared and my son had to go out and buy a new trash can. He thought he was doing me a favor by getting one with a lid attached. The next week the lid magically appeared in the middle of the street. Several weeks passed and I got a notification that my new can was too big for the service I was paying for and if I didn't stop using it I would be charged extra. Went back to the old can immediately but I got charged for the previous 3 weeks before they notified me. What a bunch of crooks.

text: Keep getting charged overweight fees while I watch 2 collectors dump two houses worth of trash into the truck at once. The can always has standard trash, nothing heavy and I've never seen the collectors struggle to pick it up. I find it hard to believe that these trucks even have a weighing system on the back of them but regardless, the way trash is collected there is no way to distinguish weigh of my trash or the house across the street (the one always dumped in the truck at the same time). Be nice if <PERSON>'s wasn't adding $10 dollar fees at their leisure.
